page.noRulesYet: No rules yet. Create one to get started.

confirmDelete.title: Delete rule
confirmDelete.message: This rule will be permanently deleted.

confirmDeleteBulk.message_one: Are you sure you want to delete {{count}} rule? This cannot be undone.
confirmDeleteBulk.message_other: Are you sure you want to delete {{count}} rules? This cannot be undone.

confirmDeleteFolder.title: Delete folder
confirmDeleteFolder.message: This will delete the folder. Rules inside won't be deleted — they'll return to the top level.

vaultCascade.moveDescription: Moving "{{folderName}}" will also move the following rules to {{targetVaultName}}:
vaultCascade.copyDescription: Copying "{{folderName}}" will also copy the following rules to {{targetVaultName}}:

ruleForm.name: Name

local.label: Local
local.title: Local tunnel
local.summary: Open something from the SSH server on your own machine.
local.example: Use localhost:3000 on your computer to reach 127.0.0.1:3000 on the server.

remote.label: Remote
remote.title: Remote tunnel
remote.summary: Expose something from your machine on the SSH server.
remote.example: The server listens on a port and forwards traffic back to your computer.

dynamic.label: Dynamic
dynamic.title: Dynamic SOCKS proxy
dynamic.summary: Create a local SOCKS5 proxy that sends app traffic through the SSH server.
dynamic.example: Point a browser or app at localhost:1080 to route traffic through the server.

ruleForm.localPortHelp: You connect to this port locally, usually as localhost:{{port}}.
ruleForm.remotePortOnServerHelp: The port where the service is running from the server's point of view.
ruleForm.hostOnServerHelp: Use 127.0.0.1 when the service runs on the SSH server itself.
ruleForm.remotePortOpenedOnServerHelp: People or services on the server side connect to this port.
ruleForm.localPortReceivingHelp: The local app or service that should receive the forwarded traffic.
ruleForm.bindAddressOnServerHelp: 127.0.0.1 keeps it private to the server. 0.0.0.0 may expose it to the network.
ruleForm.bindWarning: 0.0.0.0 exposes this port through the remote server to all its network interfaces.
ruleForm.hostOnComputerHelp: Use 127.0.0.1 when the target app runs on this computer.
ruleForm.socksProxyPort: SOCKS proxy port on your computer
ruleForm.socksProxyPortHelp: Configure apps to use localhost:{{port}} as a SOCKS5 proxy.
